exercises.title: Annotated ASCII Chart
exercises.description: Build an ASCII line chart with annotations: reference line (average), peak marker, and trend indicator. Simulate Matplotlib's annotation features using string formatting. Show monthly revenue with an average line and label the peak month.
exercises.starterCode:
def annotated_line_chart(data, title):
    """
    Print an annotated ASCII line chart.
    data: list of (month_label, value) tuples
    Annotations: average line, peak label, first/last comparison
    """
    labels = [l for l, _ in data]
    values = [v for _, v in data]
    avg = sum(values) / len(values)
    peak_idx = values.index(max(values))
    peak_label = labels[peak_idx]
    peak_val = values[peak_idx]

    print(f"{'=' * 55}")
    print(f" {title}")
    print(f"{'=' * 55}")
    print()

    # TODO: Print each month's bar with a marker
    # Format: "Jan  |█████████  $4,500"
    # Add " ← PEAK" annotation next to the peak value
    # Add " ← AVG" marker next to the bar closest to average
    max_val = max(values)
    bar_scale = 25 / max_val
    avg_idx = min(range(len(values)), key=lambda i: abs(values[i] - avg))

    for i, (label, value) in enumerate(data):
        bar_len = int(value * bar_scale)
        annotation = ""
        if i == peak_idx:
            annotation = " ← PEAK"
        elif i == avg_idx:
            annotation = f" ← AVG (${avg:,.0f})"
        # TODO: Print the row
        pass

    print()
    # TODO: Print summary statistics
    # "Average:    $X,XXX"
    # "Peak month: Month ($X,XXX)"
    # "Trend:      +X% from first to last month"
    trend_pct = (values[-1] - values[0]) / values[0] * 100
    pass

monthly_revenue = [
    ("Jan", 45000), ("Feb", 52000), ("Mar", 49000),
    ("Apr", 67000), ("May", 72000), ("Jun", 85000),
]

annotated_line_chart(monthly_revenue, "Monthly Revenue 2024")
hints: bar_len = int(value * bar_scale) where bar_scale = 25 / max_val

exercises.title: Dashboard Report Generator
exercises.description: Build a complete text-based analytics dashboard that tells a data story. Given sales data, generate a structured report with: executive summary, key metrics, regional breakdown table, trend analysis, and actionable insights. Practice composing a clear narrative from data.
exercises.starterCode:
from collections import defaultdict

sales = [
    {"month": "Jan", "region": "North", "product": "Widget", "revenue": 12000, "units": 400},
    {"month": "Jan", "region": "South", "product": "Gadget", "revenue": 8500,  "units": 170},
    {"month": "Feb", "region": "North", "product": "Widget", "revenue": 15000, "units": 500},
    {"month": "Feb", "region": "South", "product": "Gadget", "revenue": 9200,  "units": 184},
    {"month": "Mar", "region": "North", "product": "Widget", "revenue": 14000, "units": 467},
    {"month": "Mar", "region": "South", "product": "Gadget", "revenue": 11000, "units": 220},
    {"month": "Mar", "region": "East",  "product": "Widget", "revenue": 7500,  "units": 250},
]

# TODO: Compute summary metrics
total_revenue = sum(s["revenue"] for s in sales)
total_units = sum(s["units"] for s in sales)
avg_order_value = total_revenue / total_units

# TODO: Revenue by region
by_region = defaultdict(int)
for s in sales:
    by_region[s["region"]] += s["revenue"]

# TODO: Revenue by month
by_month = defaultdict(int)
for s in sales:
    by_month[s["month"]] += s["revenue"]

# TODO: Month-over-month growth
months = ["Jan", "Feb", "Mar"]
month_revenues = [by_month[m] for m in months]

# TODO: Print the full report
print("=" * 60)
print(" SALES ANALYTICS REPORT — Q1 2024")
print("=" * 60)
print()
# TODO: Print Executive Summary section
# TODO: Print Regional Breakdown table
# TODO: Print Monthly Trend section
# TODO: Print Key Insights section
exercises.expectedOutput:
============================================================
 SALES ANALYTICS REPORT — Q1 2024
============================================================

EXECUTIVE SUMMARY
-----------------
Total Revenue:  $77,200
Total Units:    2,191
Avg Unit Value: $35.24

REGIONAL BREAKDOWN
------------------
Region    Revenue     Share
North     $41,000     53.1%
South     $28,700     37.2%
East       $7,500      9.7%

MONTHLY TREND
-------------
Jan: $20,500
Feb: $24,200  (+18.0%)
Mar: $32,500  (+34.3%)

KEY INSIGHTS
------------
• North is the top region at 53.1% of revenue
• Revenue grew 58.5% from Jan to Mar
• Strongest month: Mar ($32,500)
hints: For regional share: share = by_region[r] / total_revenue * 100
For MoM growth: growth = (current - prev) / prev * 100
Insights: find top region with max(by_region, key=by_region.get)
